meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Build a Portfolio Without Experience
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to debug complex projects, prove your skills, and land high-paying clients fast.
content.answer_block: Transform your career by treating debugging as a feature, not a chore. By using AI-driven agents like Claude Code to surgically fix complex bugs in open-source projects, you create verifiable proof of expertise. This 'bug-bounty' style approach builds a robust portfolio of real-world impact without needing original side projects.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the freelancer’s silent killer. You have the technical skills, but without a track record, clients see a risk instead of a solution. The traditional advice—'build a clone'—is a waste of billable hours that leaves your portfolio looking like everyone else's. It's time to stop building toys and start fixing infrastructure.

Modern engineering isn't just about shipping code; it's about navigating the chaos of existing, broken systems. By pivoting your focus to intelligent debugging using AI agents, you demonstrate a level of seniority that most juniors lack. You aren't just a coder; you are a surgeon who can diagnose and resolve critical failures in complex environments.

steps.title: Select a High-Impact Repository
steps.description: Find an active open-source project with a backlog of 'good first issues' or performance bottlenecks. Your goal is to identify a bug that requires deep architectural understanding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this GitHub repository for performance bottlenecks and identify three critical bugs that impact user experience. Rank them by technical complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: ls -R | grep -v node_modules && claude 'Analyze the codebase structure and identify the primary entry points for the identified bug.'

steps.title: Initialize the Debugging Environment
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map dependencies and create a local sandbox. You need to isolate the failure state without breaking the production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dependency map for the target module and explain the flow of data between the UI component and the backend API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate a reproduction script that triggers the identified bug consistently in a local environment.'

steps.title: Execute Surgical Debugging
steps.description: Don't rewrite. Use AI to trace the stack and propose a minimal viable fix. This shows clients you respect existing architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a surgical code change to fix the race condition in the authentication flow while minimizing side effects on related services.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Apply the fix to the authentication module and run the existing test suite to verify no regressions occur.'

steps.title: Document the 'Why' and 'How'
steps.description: A fix without documentation is invisible. Write a detailed technical post-mortem that acts as your portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional technical case study based on this bug fix, highlighting the problem, the diagnostic process, and the impact of the solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate a markdown file named CASE_STUDY.md summarizing the bug, the debugging process, and the final solution.'

steps.title: Push and Showcase
steps.description: Submit a polished Pull Request. Even if it isn't merged immediately, the PR link is your strongest proof of work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional PR description that follows standard engineering practices, referencing the issue number and explaining the testing methodology.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'fix: resolve race condition in auth flow' && git push origin fix-auth-bug

pro_tips: Focus on 'performance regressions' rather than simple UI bugs; they command higher respect from technical leads.
Always include a 'Before vs After' performance metric in your documentation to quantify your value.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your fixes—this proves you understand the full SDLC, not just patching.
Target projects that your dream clients actually use; it makes your portfolio hyper-relevant during sales calls.
Treat your PR comments like a conversation with a senior lead; always explain the trade-offs you considered.
content.next_steps: Once your first PR is live, optimize your LinkedIn profile by adding a 'Technical Contributions' section. Link directly to your PRs and case studies to convert visitors into leads.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project to fall into your lap. Use the power of AI to build a portfolio that proves you can solve real, messy, and complex problems for paying clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
